name: HMS Wolfhound (I 56)
events: 8 Jul 1940 HMS Truant (Lt.Cdr. H.A.V. Haggard, RN) departed from Rosyth for her 10th war patrol. She is to patrol off the Norwegian coast near Bergen.
On departure A/S exercises were carried out with HMS Wallace (Cdr. B.I. Robertshaw, RN) and HMS Wolfhound (Lt.Cdr. J.W. McCoy, DSC, RN). Also a practice attack was made on HMS Wolfhound. (1)
16 Dec 1940 HMS Wolfhound (Lt.Cdr. J.W. McCoy, DSC, RN) is docked in the floating dock (AFD 3) at the Rosyth Dockyard. (2)
20 Dec 1940 HMS Wolfhound (Lt.Cdr. J.W. McCoy, DSC, RN) is undocked. (2)
22 Feb 1941 HMS Wolfhound (Lt.Cdr. J.W. McCoy, DSC, RN) and HMS Escapade (Lt.Cdr. E.N.V. Currey, DSC, RN) are docked in No.3 graving dock at the Rosyth Dockyard. (2)
8 Mar 1941 HMS Wolfhound (Lt.Cdr. J.W. McCoy, DSC, RN) and HMS Escapade (Lt.Cdr. E.N.V. Currey, DSC, RN) are undocked.
HMS Wolfhound is re-docked in No.3 graving dock at the Rosyth Dockyard together with HMS Vortigern (Lt.Cdr. R.S. Howlett, RN). (2)
19 Mar 1941 HMS Wolfhound (Lt.Cdr. J.W. McCoy, DSC, RN) and HMS Vortigern (Lt.Cdr. R.S. Howlett, RN) are undocked. (2)
3 Sep 1941 HMS Wolfhound (Lt.Cdr. J.A. Burnett, DSC, RN) is heavily damaged by near misses in a German air attack. The ships back was broken and she was out of action for over 18 months.
15 Jul 1943 HMS Wolfhound (Lt. J.H.A. Benians, DSC, RN) is docked in the floating dock (AFD 3) at the Rosyth Dockyard.
[We currently do not know when she was undocked, this was on 24 July 1943 at latest.] (2)
7 Sep 1943 HMS Wolfhound (Lt. J.H.A. Benians, DSC, RN) is docked in the floating dock (AFD 3) at the Rosyth Dockyard. (2)
8 Sep 1943 HMS Wolfhound (Lt. J.H.A. Benians, DSC, RN) is undocked. (2)
23 Jan 1944 HMS Wolfhound (Lt. J.H.A. Benians, DSC, RN) is docked in the floating dock (AFD 3) at the Rosyth Dockyard. (2)
24 Jan 1944 HMS Wolfhound (Lt. J.H.A. Benians, DSC, RN) is undocked. (2)
9 Apr 1944 HMS Royal Sovereign (Capt.(Retd.) S. Hopkins, RN) conducted speed and gunnery trials off the Firth of Forth during which she is escorted by HMS Wolfhound (Lt. J.H.A. Benians, DSC, RN). (3)
